metadata.sources: Official CFPs, author guidelines, and submission instructions (2025-2026 cycles)
metadata.note: Fields marked null could not be confirmed from public CFPs. Consult official venue pages before submission.

venues.fullName: IEEE/ACM International Conference on Software Engineering

tracks.name: Research
tracks.notes: 10 pages includes appendices/figures/tables; 2 additional pages for references only

venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template (acmart) sigconf, two-column; \documentclass[sigconf,review,anonymous]{acmart}

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: discouraged; if posted, do not say 'submitted to ICSE'

deskRejectCriteria: Wrong template / decreased margins or font

authorshipPolicy.plagiarismPolicy: ACM Policy and Procedures on Plagiarism + IEEE Plagiarism FAQ
authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: Concurrent submission deemed serious breach of ethics
authorshipPolicy.notes: Two cycles per year; ~8-week revision window in cycle 1

dates.notes: Cycle 1: Mar 15; Cycle 2: Jul 19; Major revision deadline: Nov 15

venues.fullName: ACM International Conference on the Foundations of Software Engineering

tracks.name: Research
tracks.notes: PACMSE single-column acmsmall format; submission body 18 pages + 4 ref pages; major revision body limit 20 pages + 4 ref pages

venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template, single-column acmsmall (PACMSE) — \documentclass[acmsmall,screen,review,anonymous]{acmart}

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: recommended to delay; if posted, don't disclose FSE submission

deskRejectCriteria: Use of non-double-column or wrong template style

authorshipPolicy.plagiarismPolicy: ACM Policy and Procedures on Plagiarism
authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: No concurrent submission; standard ACM rules

dates.notes: Cycle 1 deadline 11 Sep 2025 AoE; two cycles per year. Author/title list cannot change after submission.

other: GenAI use disclosure requested at submission

venues.fullName: IEEE/ACM International Conference on Automated Software Engineering
venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template, sigconf two-column

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed but should not identify ASE submission

venues.fullName: ACM SIGSOFT International Symposium on Software Testing and Analysis

tracks.name: Research
tracks.notes: Major revision body limit 20 pages

deskRejectCriteria: Strict 18-page limit; >18 pages (anything except references) → desk reject

specialRequiredSections.name: Data Availability
specialRequiredSections.notes: Mandatory since ISSTA 2024, does not count toward limit

dates.notes: ISSTA 2025 was 25-28 June in Trondheim, Norway. ISSTA 2026 dates TBA — verify against the live CFP. Strict 18-page enforcement; >18 pages of non-reference content (text+figures+appendix) → desk reject.

supplementaryPolicy.notes: At least one reviewer will check supplementary

venues.fullName: International Conference on Mining Software Repositories
venues.edition: MSR 2026, Rio de Janeiro (co-located with ICSE)
venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template, sigconf, double-column

venues.fullName: Annual Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems

tracks.name: Main (Research)
tracks.notes: 9 content pages at submission; 10 at camera-ready (one extra page on acceptance). Checklist does not count toward limit. Contribution types: General, Theory, Use-Inspired, Concept & Feasibility, Negative Results.

tracks.name: Evaluations & Datasets
tracks.notes: Renamed from 'Datasets & Benchmarks' for 2026. Follows main-track CFP (no separate page-limit). Code release required at submission for benchmark/evaluation-environment artifacts.

tracks.name: Position Paper
tracks.notes: Second year for the track; double-blind; rationale for fit to track required. No checklist required.

abstract.notes: Placeholder abstract forbidden; rewriting at full paper deadline forbidden

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed; papers appearing online after March 1, 2026 are considered contemporaneous work and not grounds for novelty rejection

deskRejectCriteria: Page limit exceeded / margin or font tampering
Concurrent submission to another archival venue

referenceFormat.notes: Author's choice, must be consistent; 9pt font allowed

authorshipPolicy.plagiarismPolicy: NeurIPS Code of Conduct prohibits plagiarism
authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: Concurrent submission to other archival venues prohibited

dates.notes: Sydney International Convention Centre. All authors must have an OpenReview profile by full-paper deadline.

other: LLM use disclosure if LLM is component of methods
Contribution types tagged at submission (General/Theory/Use-Inspired/Concept & Feasibility/Negative Results)

venues.fullName: International Conference on Machine Learning

tracks.name: Main (Research)
tracks.notes: 8 content pages at submission (over-length → automatic rejection); 9 at camera-ready. Impact statement and references unlimited.

abstract.notes: ~4-6 sentences; abstract pre-registration required (Jan 24, 2026 AoE) one week before full paper deadline

deskRejectCriteria: Lack of broader-impact statement in camera-ready

authorshipPolicy.notes: Reciprocal reviewing: authors with multiple submissions required to review

dates.notes: COEX Seoul. Reciprocal-reviewing: authors with 4+ submissions must serve as reviewer (threshold may drop to 3).

venues.fullName: International Conference on Learning Representations

tracks.name: Main (Research)
tracks.notes: Ethics statement does not count toward limit

reviewPolicy.socialMediaDiscussion: permitted; reviews are anonymous and publicly visible

deskRejectCriteria: Main text beyond page limit (e.g., 11th page)

venues.fullName: AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence
deskRejectCriteria: Concurrent submission with another archival venue
Modifications to paper after full-paper deadline

authorshipPolicy.plagiarismPolicy: AAAI Code of Professional Ethics; sanctions include retraction and ban
authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: No paper under review/accepted in archival venue; arXiv & workshops OK
authorshipPolicy.notes: Two-phase review: Phase 1 reject → final; Phase 2 accept/reject after rebuttal. AI-Assisted Peer Review pilot.

venues.fullName: International Joint Conference on Artificial Intelligence

tracks.name: Main Track
tracks.notes: 7 body + 2 refs = 9 total. Optional ethics statement may be placed in body or in the 2 reference pages.

abstract.notes: Abstract registration required (Jan 12, 2026) one week before full paper. No official word limit; ~200 words recommended.

authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: No prior or concurrent in formal venues; workshops without proceedings OK
authorshipPolicy.notes: Two-phase reviewing; IJCAI-ECAI 2026 requires resubmission disclosure

dates.notes: Bremen, Germany. Five special tracks alongside main: Human-Centered AI, AI for Social Good, AI4Tech, AI and Health, AI and Robotics — all share 7+2 page format.

other: Resubmission disclosure required (12-month window): attach prior reviews + response letter
Max 8 submissions per author across all tracks (Primary Paper Initiative)
LLM-detection tools used; LLM-written papers desk-rejected

venues.fullName: Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics

tracks.name: Long Papers
tracks.notes: 8 content pages at submission; 9 at camera-ready. References and appendices unlimited.

venues.template: ACL Rolling Review LaTeX (Overleaf [review] setting on)
deskRejectCriteria: Appendices not in double-column format (since July 2025)
Submissions attempting to manipulate machine readers (e.g., hidden prompts)

authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: ARR Multiple Submission Policy; >25% overlap → reject
authorshipPolicy.notes: ARR workflow: submit → review → commit to venue

dates.notes: ARR-only submission: eligible cycles ARR October 2025 and ARR January 2026, then commit to ACL 2026 via OpenReview commitment site. Papers automatically considered for Findings of the ACL.

other: Responsible NLP Checklist mandatory (made public for accepted papers)
ARR workflow (no separate ACL abstract pre-registration deadline)
TACL/CL journal-track papers presented at conference alongside ACL submissions
Irresponsible reviewers may be ineligible to submit

venues.fullName: Conference on Empirical Methods in Natural Language Processing
ACM SIGIR Conference on Research and Development in Information Retrieval

tracks.name: Resource & Reproducibility
tracks.notes: Single-blind review

venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template (sigconf for LaTeX) — two-column

authorshipPolicy.notes: SIGIR-RR option to ICTIR/SIGIR-AP for unaccepted papers

venues.fullName: ACM SIGKDD Conference on Knowledge Discovery and Data Mining

tracks.name: Research Track
tracks.notes: Camera-ready total max 12 pages (9 content + 3 appendix)

venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template, sigconf, anonymous, review

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed; no citation of own arXiv preprint

deskRejectCriteria: Concurrent submission to peer-reviewed venue with proceedings

authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: No concurrent or accepted; arXiv/SSRN allowed
authorshipPolicy.notes: Two cycles per year; Resubmit carries to next cycle with 1-page changes summary

dates.notes: Two cycles per year. Cycle 1: abstract Jul 24, full paper Jul 31, 2025. Cycle 2: abstract Feb 1, full paper Feb 8, 2026. Two-week author rebuttal period. All accepted papers' submissions, reviews, meta-reviews, and discussions become public on OpenReview.

other: Max 7 submissions per author per cycle
New for 2026: Datasets and Benchmarks Track, AI for Sciences Track
Resubmit decisions can carry to next cycle; rejected/withdrawn cannot

venues.fullName: The ACM Web Conference (formerly WWW)

tracks.name: Research
tracks.notes: Max 12 total pages; first 8 must be self-contained; reviewers not required to read past page 8

venues.template: ACM Primary Article Template, sigconf, anonymous, review

abstract.notes: Placeholder forbidden; first page must state relevance to the Web

deskRejectCriteria: Submissions that don't state relevance to the Web on first page
Failure of ACM Human Subjects Policy
conventionalSections: Ethical use of data / informed consent

other: Web relevance statement required on first page

venues.fullName: IEEE International Conference on Software Maintenance and Evolution

reviewPolicy.selfCitationPolicy: must use third person; may anonymize if would unblind
reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed; do not cite as own

authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: Must not be under review or published elsewhere
authorshipPolicy.notes: ICSME 2026 introduces early decisions (some papers accepted/rejected before rebuttal)

other: Best papers invited to EMSE special issue

venues.fullName: IEEE International Conference on Software Analysis, Evolution, and Reengineering

reviewPolicy.selfCitationPolicy: must use third person; can anonymize references if would unblind

other: Best papers invited to EMSE special issue

venues.fullName: ACM/IEEE International Symposium on Empirical Software Engineering and Measurement
venues.template: Standard IEEE conference template (2025); publisher transition in progress for 2026

abstract.notes: ESEM REQUIRES a structured abstract — desk reject if missing

dates.notes: ESEM 2026 dates TBA; publisher transition in progress

venues.fullName: IEEE International Conference on Software Testing, Verification and Validation
IEEE International Requirements Engineering Conference
ACM/IEEE International Conference on Model-Driven Engineering Languages and Systems

tracks.name: Foundations (Research)
tracks.notes: Double-anonymous

tracks.name: Practice
tracks.notes: Single-anonymous

venues.template: IEEE conference format with mandatory ORCIDs

other: SoSyM journal special-issue invitations for best papers

venues.fullName: IEEE/ACM International Conference on Program Comprehension

tracks.name: Research
tracks.notes: Double-anonymous

tracks.name: RENE
tracks.notes: Single-blind

other: EMSE special issue for select papers

venues.fullName: International Conference on Evaluation and Assessment in Software Engineering

tracks.name: Research Papers
tracks.notes: Everything inclusive — refs count toward 10 pages

reviewPolicy.notes: Mandatory SIGSOFT Open Science Policy — artifacts must be in permanent repositories (Zenodo, not GitHub)

venues.fullName: IEEE Transactions on Software Engineering

tracks.name: Regular Paper
tracks.notes: ~14 pages IEEE Transactions two-column; over-length charges apply

authorshipPolicy.overlapPolicy: No concurrent submission; 2-month embargo after rejection

dates.notes: Continuously open; invites extended versions of distinguished ICSE/ISSTA/FSE papers

venues.fullName: ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology

tracks.name: Regular Paper
tracks.notes: ~11,000 words soft cap; may be returned without review if exceeded

deskRejectCriteria: Incomplete / over page limit / incorrectly formatted

dates.notes: Continuously open; from Jan 1 2026 all-OA with APC requirement

other: Special sections on Sustainable SE, Quantum SE

venues.fullName: Empirical Software Engineering — An International Journal

tracks.name: Regular Paper
tracks.notes: No strict page limit; extensive papers expected to justify length

dates.notes: Aim for first decision in <3 months

other: Partner journal for ICSME/ESEM/SANER Registered Reports

venues.fullName: Journal of Systems and Software

tracks.name: Regular Research Paper
tracks.notes: No fixed page limit

tracks.name: Practitioner Insights
tracks.notes: First author must be from industry

referenceFormat.notes: Numbered or author-year depending on template choice

venues.fullName: Information and Software Technology

tracks.name: Regular Research Paper
tracks.notes: No strict limit

other: Premier outlet for SE systematic reviews

venues.fullName: Annual Conference of the North American Chapter of the ACL

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed; no anonymity period since early 2024

deskRejectCriteria: Missing or incorrect Responsible NLP Checklist

dates.notes: Uses ARR; Oct 2024 cycle for NAACL 2025

other: Irresponsible reviewers may be ineligible to submit

venues.fullName: International Conference on Computational Linguistics
venues.edition: COLING 2025, Abu Dhabi; next: COLING 2027, Macau

reviewPolicy.notes: Accepts both direct submissions AND ARR commitments

deskRejectCriteria: Deviation from style files (auto-reject without review)
Dual submission to multiple COLING tracks

venues.fullName: Conference of the European Chapter of the ACL

reviewPolicy.arxivDuringReview: allowed; no anonymity period since Feb 2024
reviewPolicy.system: ACL Rolling Review (ARR) only — no direct submission

venues.fullName: ACM International Conference on Information and Knowledge Management

tracks.name: Full Research Papers
tracks.notes: GenAI Usage Disclosure section does not count

deskRejectCriteria: Text generated by LLMs (unless experimental analysis)

authorshipPolicy.notes: LLM-produced text in body PROHIBITED unless part of experimental analysis

other: Author list locked after abstract deadline

venues.fullName: ACM International Conference on Web Search and Data Mining

tracks.name: Full Research Paper
tracks.notes: ACM sigconf format; exact page limit not explicitly stated

tracks.name: Short Research Paper
tracks.notes: NEW for WSDM 2026 — first short paper track in series

authorshipPolicy.plagiarismPolicy: ACM strict policy on plagiarism and self-plagiarism

other: In-person required; proxies must present if author cannot

venues.fullName: European Conference on Information Retrieval

tracks.name: Full Paper
tracks.notes: Appendix before references

venues.template: Springer LNCS (LaTeX or Word), with ORCID encouraged

specialRequiredSections.name: Theory of Change
specialRequiredSections.notes: Mandatory only in IR-for-Good track for tool/method/resource papers

other: Consent to publish required for Springer LNCS
